apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - security.openshift.io
  resourceNames:
  - privileged
  resources:
  - securitycontextconstraints
  verbs:
  - use
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  managedFields:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: privileged-rights
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: builder
  namespace: openshift-ptp
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman
  namespace: openshift-ptp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  serviceAccountName: builder
  containers:
    - name: priv
      image: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4
      command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=PTP_IMAGE
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch OPERATOR_VERSION https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/redhat-cne/cloud-event-proxy.git
          cd cloud-event-proxy
          IMG=${SIDECAR_IMG} make podman-build
          podman push ${SIDECAR_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

      securityContext:
        privileged: true
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push
          name: dockercfg
          readOnly: true
        - name: secret-volume
          mountPath: /root/.docker
  volumes:
    - name: secret-volume
      secret:
        secretName: pull-secret
        items:
        - key: .dockerconfigjson
          path: config.json

    - name: dockercfg
      secret:
        secretName: BUILDER_DOCKERCFG
        defaultMode: 384
'

            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=PTP_IMAGE
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch release-4.17 https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/redhat-cne/cloud-event-proxy.git
          cd cloud-event-proxy
          IMG=${SIDECAR_IMG} make podman-build
          podman push ${SIDECAR_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

      securityContext:
        privileged: true
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push
          name: dockercfg
          readOnly: true
        - name: secret-volume
          mountPath: /root/.docker
  volumes:
    - name: secret-volume
      secret:
        secretName: pull-secret
        items:
        - key: .dockerconfigjson
          path: config.json

    - name: dockercfg
      secret:
        secretName: BUILDER_DOCKERCFG
        defaultMode: 384'

          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch release-4.17 https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch

issues.ai_summary:
Now I have enough information to write the complete analysis. Let me compile everything:

---

### Test 1: `[PTP e2e integration tests][bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on single NIC boundary clock [Serial]`

- **Duration:** 378.998s (~6 min 19 sec)
- **Error:** `Expected PMC clock class 248 for ptp4l.0.config` or `Expected node-level clock class metric 248` — the test timed out in its 5-minute `Eventually` waiting for `openshift_ptp_clock_class` to change from `6` (locked) to `248` (freerun) after the slave interface was brought down.
- **Root Cause:** The test (`main` branch of ptp-operator, added for OCPBUGS-66407/59883) brings down the BC's slave interface, then waits up to 5 minutes for `ptp4l` to transition clock class from `6` (Locked) → `248` (Freerun/Default) and for the `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric to reflect this. The `linuxptp-daemon:release-4.17` does not update this metric on upstream sync loss within the test window. Specifically, `checkClockClassState` at `ptp.go:3121` and `verifyNodeClockClassWithMetrics` at `ptptesthelper.go:745` poll the local metrics endpoint with a `clockClassRe` regex that expects the metric to change; it never does on 4.17. The `EnableGMCapableInPlace` function modifies the BC ptp4l config in-place, but the 4.17 daemon may not fully propagate this to a state change observable via metrics.
- **Breaking PR/Commit:** The test was introduced in ptp-operator main branch via commits related to OCPBUGS-66407. Commit `3586177d40d8` (May 19 2026, "ci: add ginkgo flake mitigation and fix higher-priority test") and prior commits `5002071b6f51`/`46769f59141a` (March 25 2026, "Fix test structure and add miscellaneous helpers / WriteStep logging") added or hardened this test. The root issue is that these `main`-branch tests were never validated against a `release-4.17` daemon. Breaking PR: https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/5002071b6f51 (adds `checkClockClassState` timeout parameter and the Verify clockClass test logic); origin in OCPBUGS-66407.
- **Source File:** https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1121
- **Is it a flake?** No — the test ran for the full ~6 minutes, which is a deterministic timeout. The same pattern appears across bc-serial (and similar patterns in other suites). A flake would resolve on retry; here the 4.17 daemon consistently does not emit the expected clock class transition.
- **Suggested Fix:** Cherry-pick the `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric update-on-sync-loss fix from `main`/`release-4.18` into `release-4.17`, OR add a version gate in the test (at `ptptesthelper.go:765`) to skip the clockClass metric verification on OCP < 4.18 for this specific test. The `VerifyNICClockClass` function already has a version branch at `ptptesthelper.go:765`; extend it to also skip `VerifyClockClassViaPMC` if the 4.17 daemon's ptp4l PMC data doesn't reflect the expected transition.

---

### Test 2: `[PTP e2e integration tests][bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP Outage recovery BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage [Serial]`

- **Duration:** 382.759s (~6 min 23 sec)
- **Error:** `expected clock class to degrade from Locked (6) after upstream link loss` — the 5-minute `Eventually` at `ptp.go:2153` timed out waiting for `checkClockClassStateReturnBool` to return `false` (i.e., clock class not equal to 6). After all slave interfaces were brought down and confirmed FAULTY, `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric remained at value `6` and never transitioned to a degraded value.
- **Root Cause:** `BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage` (introduced in `main` branch, `ptp.go:2086`) uses `portEngine.TurnAllPortsDown` to bring all BC slave interfaces down, waits for FAULTY role via `metrics.CheckClockRole`, then checks that the `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric degrades away from `6`. The `release-4.17` linuxptp-daemon's `UpdateClockClassMetrics` function

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - security.openshift.io
  resourceNames:
  - privileged
  resources:
  - securitycontextconstraints
  verbs:
  - use
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  managedFields:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: privileged-rights
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: builder
  namespace: openshift-ptp
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman
  namespace: openshift-ptp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  serviceAccountName: builder
  containers:
    - name: priv
      image: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4
      command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=PTP_IMAGE
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch OPERATOR_VERSION https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/redhat-cne/cloud-event-proxy.git
          cd cloud-event-proxy
          IMG=${SIDECAR_IMG} make podman-build
          podman push ${SIDECAR_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

      securityContext:
        privileged: true
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push
          name: dockercfg
          readOnly: true
        - name: secret-volume
          mountPath: /root/.docker
  volumes:
    - name: secret-volume
      secret:
        secretName: pull-secret
        items:
        - key: .dockerconfigjson
          path: config.json

    - name: dockercfg
      secret:
        secretName: BUILDER_DOCKERCFG
        defaultMode: 384
'

            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch release-4.18 https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --b

Now I have all the evidence I need to write the complete analysis.

---

### Test 1: `[telco5g-ptp-tests] Run multi-stage test e2e-telco5g-ptp - e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests container test`

- **Duration:** ~10m08s (step started at `2026-06-11T20:50:57Z`, failed at `2026-06-11T21:01:09Z`). The inner `retry_with_timeout` polling loop ran for the full 400-second budget (timestamps `1781211267` → `1781211667`).

- **Error:** `Error from server (NotFound): daemonsets.apps "linuxptp-daemon" not found` — emitted on every 5-second poll for 400 seconds without the DaemonSet ever appearing. The wait is at line 956 of the test step script:
  ```bash
  retry_with_timeout 400 5 kubectl rollout status daemonset linuxptp-daemon -nopenshift-ptp
  ```

- **Root Cause:** The failure is a cascading build-image failure. Inside `build_images()`, the `podman` pod runs `make docker-build` which builds the ptp-operator image from `Dockerfile`:
  ```
  FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18 AS builder
  ```
  The CI builder image returned `manifest unknown` — the tag `rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18` does not exist in `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder` at the time of this run (likely pruned as OCP 4.18 nightly builds age out). `make docker-build` fails with exit code 125, but due to a bash `set -e` exception inside `if-then-fi` compound commands, the pod script continues and reaches `exit 0` (the `USE_UPSTREAM=false` early-exit path at line 719 of the script). The pod reports phase `Succeeded`. The outer `build_images()` function prints `[INFO] index build succeeded` and continues. The ptp-operator is then deployed via `make deploy IMG=...ptp-operator:4.18`, but that image tag was never pushed to the internal registry. The ptp-operator Deployment is created (log line 6160: `deployment.apps/ptp-operator created`) but the pod enters `ImagePullBackOff`. Without the operator running, `PtpOperatorConfigReconciler.syncLinuxptpDaemon()` in `controllers/ptpoperatorconfig_controller.go` never executes, the `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet is never created, and the wait loop times out.

- **Breaking PR/Commit:** Not a code regression in ptp-operator itself. The breakage is caused by the CI builder image `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18` no longer being available — likely because 4.18 nightly promotion stopped producing fresh builder images. The most recent change to `release-4.18` (PR [#670](https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/670), merged 2026-03-17, commit `ecb9174`) only touched `Dockerfile.ocp` (changed `GO111MODULE=off` → `GO111MODULE=auto`), but that file is not used for 4.18 builds — the CI script always takes the `Dockerfile` path for `T5CI_VERSION=4.18`. The secondary contributing bug is in the `build_images()` function of the test step script, which silently treats a failed build pod as a success.

- **Source File:**
  - Test step (wait loop): `https://github.com/openshift/release/blob/master/ci-operator/step-registry/telco5g/ptp/tests/telco5g-ptp-tests-commands.sh#L956`
  - Operator controller (DaemonSet creation): `https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-4.18/controllers/ptpoperatorconfig_controller.go` (`syncLinuxptpDaemon`, renders `bindata/linuxptp/ptp-daemon.yaml`)

- **Is it a flake?** No — the builder image `manifest unknown` error is deterministic; the tag no longer exists in `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder`. This will reproduce on every run until the builder image is restored or the `Dockerfile` is updated to use a currently available base image. There is no evidence of this test flaking for timing or sync reasons.

- **Suggested Fix:**
  1. **Immediate:** Update `Dockerfile` in the `release-4.18` branch to use an available builder image (e.g., the RHEL 9 + Go 1.22 image from the OCP 4.18 payload, or a stable alternative like `quay.io/projectquay/golang:1.22`). Alternatively, adopt `Dockerfile.ocp` (which uses `GO111MODULE=a

The evidence is clear and complete. There are no JUnit XML files or test result files because the job never reached the test execution phase. Here is the full analysis:

---

**No individual test failures to analyze.**

The job `periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.20-e2e-telco5g-ptp` (build `2065298224968634368`) did not run any tests. It failed at the **CI infrastructure input-resolution stage**, before any cluster was provisioned or any test binary was executed.

The job ran for only 15 seconds before reporting failure with reason `resolving_inputs:resolving_release`.

**Exact error:**
```
could not resolve inputs: could not determine inputs for step [release:initial]:
  failed to resolve release initial:
    failed to request latest release:
      Get "https://amd64.ocp.releases.ci.openshift.org/api/v1/releasestream/4.20.0-0.nightly/latest?rel=1":
      GET https://amd64.ocp.releases.ci.openshift.org/api/v1/releasestream/4.20.0-0.nightly/latest?rel=1
      giving up after 5 attempt(s)
```

The OpenShift release controller at `amd64.ocp.releases.ci.openshift.org` was either unavailable, returning errors, or had no valid 4.20.0-0.nightly payload published at the time this periodic job ran (2026-06-12 05:01 UTC). The Prow step resolver retried 5 times and gave up.

---

**Relation Between Failures:** There are no individual test failures — this is a single, atomic infra-level failure. No test code from `ptp-operator`, `linuxptp-daemon`, or `cloud-event-proxy` was involved.

**Per-Version Notes:** This failure is specific to the 4.20 nightly stream. If the 4.20 nightly payload was temporarily absent or the release controller endpoint was transiently unavailable, re-running the job when a valid nightly exists would succeed. Other versions (4.17, 4.18, etc.) use separate release streams and would not be affected by this specific incident.

**Affected Images:**
- No images were built or used — the job failed before image resolution.
- The attempted image source: `amd64.ocp.releases.ci.openshift.org` / stream `4.20.0-0.nightly`

**Overall Issue Class:** `infra_other` — specifically a transient or sustained unavailability of the OCP 4.20 nightly release controller endpoint, causing the Prow job to fail at input resolution with no test execution.

**Overall Severity:** LOW — This is a transient CI infrastructure issue, not a regression in PTP operator code or tests. No test actually failed; the job simply could not start. Re-running the periodic job once a valid 4.20 nightly payload is available should recover automatically. No source code changes are required.

---

**Failed tests:** (none extracted)

The job `periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.22-e2e-telco5g-ptp` did not produce any failed tests because it never reached the test execution phase. The entire job was aborted at the input resolution step. Per the instructions to investigate each failed test individually — there are no individual test failures to analyze.

---

**Relation Between Failures:** There are no test-level failures to relate. The single failure mode is a pre-run infrastructure error that prevented the test suite from starting entirely.

**Per-Version Notes:** Only OCP 4.22 is in scope. The failure is specific to the 4.22.0-0.nightly release stream endpoint being unreachable; there is no indication of a code-level regression in the ptp-operator, linuxptp-daemon, or cloud-event-proxy source trees.

**Affected Images:**
- None — no images were pulled or tested. The job failed before any image resolution or cluster provisioning occurred.

**Overall Issue Class:** `infra_other`

**Root cause detail:** The Prow job step runner attempted to resolve the `release:initial` input by querying the OpenShift release controller API at `https://amd64.ocp.releases.ci.openshift.org/api/v1/releasestream/4.22.0-0.nightly/latest?rel=1`. That HTTP GET failed on all 5 retry attempts. This is a transient or sustained unavailability of the release controller service (or a network path to it from the Prow build cluster), not a bug in the PTP operator or its test suite. The job exited with reason `resolving_inputs:resolving_release` after ~15 seconds — well before any e2e step ran.

**Overall Severity:** LOW — This is a transient CI infrastructure connectivity failure with no evidence of a source-code regression. The PTP operator test suite was not executed at all; re-running the job once the release controller is healthy is the correct remediation. If this failure recurs consistently across multiple nightly runs it should be escalated to the OpenShift CI infrastructure team to investigate the release controller availability for the 4.22.0-0.nightly stream.

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - security.openshift.io
  resourceNames:
  - privileged
  resources:
  - securitycontextconstraints
  verbs:
  - use
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  managedFields:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: privileged-rights
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: builder
  namespace: openshift-ptp
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman
  namespace: openshift-ptp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  serviceAccountName: builder
  containers:
    - name: priv
      image: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4
      command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=PTP_IMAGE
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch OPERATOR_VERSION https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/redhat-cne/cloud-event-proxy.git
          cd cloud-event-proxy
          IMG=${SIDECAR_IMG} make podman-build
          podman push ${SIDECAR_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

      securityContext:
        privileged: true
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push
          name: dockercfg
          readOnly: true
        - name: secret-volume
          mountPath: /root/.docker
  volumes:
    - name: secret-volume
      secret:
        secretName: pull-secret
        items:
        - key: .dockerconfigjson
          path: config.json

    - name: dockercfg
      secret:
        secretName: BUILDER_DOCKERCFG
        defaultMode: 384
'

              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/redhat-cne/cloud-event-proxy.git

issues.ai_summary:
Now I have enough information to write the complete analysis. Let me compile everything:

---

### Test 1: `[PTP e2e integration tests][bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on single NIC boundary clock [Serial]`

- **Duration:** 378.998s (~6 min 19 sec)
- **Error:** `Expected PMC clock class 248 for ptp4l.0.config` or `Expected node-level clock class metric 248` — the test timed out in its 5-minute `Eventually` waiting for `openshift_ptp_clock_class` to change from `6` (locked) to `248` (freerun) after the slave interface was brought down.
- **Root Cause:** The test (`main` branch of ptp-operator, added for OCPBUGS-66407/59883) brings down the BC's slave interface, then waits up to 5 minutes for `ptp4l` to transition clock class from `6` (Locked) → `248` (Freerun/Default) and for the `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric to reflect this. The `linuxptp-daemon:release-4.17` does not update this metric on upstream sync loss within the test window. Specifically, `checkClockClassState` at `ptp.go:3121` and `verifyNodeClockClassWithMetrics` at `ptptesthelper.go:745` poll the local metrics endpoint with a `clockClassRe` regex that expects the metric to change; it never does on 4.17. The `EnableGMCapableInPlace` function modifies the BC ptp4l config in-place, but the 4.17 daemon may not fully propagate this to a state change observable via metrics.
- **Breaking PR/Commit:** The test was introduced in ptp-operator main branch via commits related to OCPBUGS-66407. Commit `3586177d40d8` (May 19 2026, "ci: add ginkgo flake mitigation and fix higher-priority test") and prior commits `5002071b6f51`/`46769f59141a` (March 25 2026, "Fix test structure and add miscellaneous helpers / WriteStep logging") added or hardened this test. The root issue is that these `main`-branch tests were never validated against a `release-4.17` daemon. Breaking PR: https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/5002071b6f51 (adds `checkClockClassState` timeout parameter and the Verify clockClass test logic); origin in OCPBUGS-66407.
- **Source File:** https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1121
- **Is it a flake?** No — the test ran for the full ~6 minutes, which is a deterministic timeout. The same pattern appears across bc-serial (and similar patterns in other suites). A flake would resolve on retry; here the 4.17 daemon consistently does not emit the expected clock class transition.
- **Suggested Fix:** Cherry-pick the `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric update-on-sync-loss fix from `main`/`release-4.18` into `release-4.17`, OR add a version gate in the test (at `ptptesthelper.go:765`) to skip the clockClass metric verification on OCP < 4.18 for this specific test. The `VerifyNICClockClass` function already has a version branch at `ptptesthelper.go:765`; extend it to also skip `VerifyClockClassViaPMC` if the 4.17 daemon's ptp4l PMC data doesn't reflect the expected transition.

---

### Test 2: `[PTP e2e integration tests][bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP Outage recovery BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage [Serial]`

- **Duration:** 382.759s (~6 min 23 sec)
- **Error:** `expected clock class to degrade from Locked (6) after upstream link loss` — the 5-minute `Eventually` at `ptp.go:2153` timed out waiting for `checkClockClassStateReturnBool` to return `false` (i.e., clock class not equal to 6). After all slave interfaces were brought down and confirmed FAULTY, `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric remained at value `6` and never transitioned to a degraded value.
- **Root Cause:** `BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage` (introduced in `main` branch, `ptp.go:2086`) uses `portEngine.TurnAllPortsDown` to bring all BC slave interfaces down, waits for FAULTY role via `metrics.CheckClockRole`, then checks that the `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric degrades away from `6`. The `release-4.17` linuxptp-daemon's `UpdateClockClassMetrics` function

analysis.ai_summary:
**Root Cause:** The `e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream-telco5g-ptp-tests` step aborted during its pre-flight node readiness check before any PTP tests ran. Worker node `cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com` was `NotReady,SchedulingDisabled` with a Ready condition of `Unknown`, while the other workers and all masters were Ready. The test script enforces that every node must be Ready before starting PTP tests and exited with code 1. Post-failure gather data shows cluster instability: 6 Running Machines but only 5 Ready/Schedulable nodes, and a worker (`cnfdf32`) was still coming up (8m36s old at test time). This points to bare-metal telco5g CI cluster provisioning/rotation not finishing before tests started — not a PTP operator defect.

**Is it a flake?** yes — The failure is environmental. Cluster setup (`telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup`) succeeded, but a worker never reached Ready in time. The cluster was mid-provisioning (new worker `cnfdf32` joining while `cnfdf30` stayed NotReady). Symptom detection found no panics, segfaults, or quota issues. No PTP operator code was exercised; the job never reached conformance/e2e test execution.

**Suggested Fix:** Re-run the rehearse job — it will likely pass once all worker nodes are Ready. If this recurs, address it in the openshift/release CI config (`telco5g-ptp-tests` step): add a retry/wait loop for all nodes to reach Ready (with timeout) instead of failing immediately, and/or ensure cluster setup waits for full node readiness before the test phase. No change is needed in the ptp-operator codebase.

**Fix Written:** no

**Severity:** MEDIUM — CI rehearsal blocked with no evidence of a product bug; impact is limited to CI signal on openshift/release PR 80182, not PTP operator functionality.

The build log has all the information needed. Here is the full analysis:

---

The CI job ran on **2026-06-08** against OCP **4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-06-07-152904**. The multi-stage test pipeline started its `pre` phase (cluster setup) successfully, but the `test` phase was aborted immediately — **no PTP tests executed at all**. Because no individual test cases ran, the JUnit/test_results files contain no test-level failures, which is why the evidence summary said "No meaningful output found in logs."

### Test 1: `[e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream] telco5g-ptp-tests step (pre-flight node readiness check)`

- **Duration:** ~10 seconds (step ran 8–10 s and then exited)
- **Error:**

  ```
  [ERROR] The following nodes are not Ready:
  cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com Unknown
  [ERROR] All nodes must be Ready before starting PTP tests. Aborting.
  exit 1
  ```
  Container `test` in pod `e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream-telco5g-ptp-tests` exited with code 1.
- **Root Cause:** The bare-metal worker node `cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com` was in state `NotReady,SchedulingDisabled` (Kubernetes Ready condition reported `Unknown`) when the test script checked node health. The `telco5g-ptp-tests-commands.sh` step-registry script performs an explicit pre-flight gate — it queries `oc get nodes` and aborts if any node is not in `Ready=True` state. Node `cnfdf30` had been in the `Ready` state only 15 minutes prior to test start (vs. 8 m36 s for `cnfdf32`, suggesting a reboot/reprovisioning happened mid-setup), and its status flipped to `Unknown` (connectivity lost to kubelet) before the test step fired. This is a transient infra condition, not a code defect in ptp-operator or linuxptp-daemon.
- **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — this is not a code regression. It is a physical/virtual lab node going unreachable. No source change caused it.
- **Source File:** The node-readiness gate lives in the OpenShift Release step-registry script: `https://github.com/openshift/release/blob/main/ci-operator/step-registry/telco5g/ptp/tests/telco5g-ptp-tests-commands.sh` (the exact `oc get nodes … grep -v 'True$'` block). There is no corresponding ptp-operator source file because no test code ran.
- **Is it a flake?** Yes — with evidence. The failure happened within 10 seconds of the test step starting, before any PTP-specific logic executed. The node `cnfdf30` was `NotReady,SchedulingDisabled` with status `Unknown`, which is a transient lab-hardware / network-connectivity symptom (common in bare-metal telco CI pools after a node reboot or BMC/IPMI timeout). The other two worker nodes (`cnfdf31`, `cnfdf32`) were fully `Ready`. No changes to ptp-operator, linuxptp-daemon, or cloud-event-proxy are implicated.
- **Suggested Fix:** Re-run the job — if `cnfdf30` recovers, tests will proceed normally. If the node is persistently unhealthy, the telco5g lab team should investigate the BMC/IPMI connectivity and reprovisioning for `cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com`. Optionally, add a retry/wait loop (e.g., `oc wait node cnfdf30 --for=condition=Ready --timeout=5m`) before the hard abort to tolerate brief post-provisioning delays.

---

**Relation Between Failures:** There is only one failure event: the pre-flight node-readiness check. No individual PTP test cases ran, so there are no multiple independent test failures to correlate. The single abort was caused entirely by one bare-metal node being unreachable at job start time.

**Per-Version Notes:** Only OCP 4.22 is covered by this job. There is no evidence of the same failure in other versions from this run. The root cause is infrastructure-specific to the telco5g lab node pool, not an OCP-version-specific code issue.

**Affected Images:**
- No ptp-operator or linuxptp-daemon images were exercised (tests never started). The test runner image `ci/telco-runner:latest` was the only image used at the point of failure.

**Overall Issue Class:** `infra_other` — bare-metal worker node entered `NotReady/Unknown` state before

analysis.reason: Test failed: RETRYING: [10.8.34.218]: Try to grab file to see if the installation has finished (150 retries left).
analysis.ai_summary:
**Root Cause:** OpenShift 4.12 bare-metal cluster installation failed during the telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup pre-phase. The ansible playbook on lab host 10.8.34.218 deployed cluster ptpcimno via kcli but openshift-installer never completed: /root/cluster_ready.txt was never created after 150 retries (~2h38m). ClusterVersion 4.12.0-0.nightly-2026-06-05-032308 remained Progressing with MultipleErrors for 144+ minutes — all 28+ cluster operators unavailable, oc get nodes returned no resources, and the API server at 10.8.34.117:6443 eventually became unreachable. PTP e2e tests never ran.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** Not identifiable in openshift/ptp-operator. Failure is in openshift-installer/cluster provisioning on the telco bare-metal lab (openshift/release CI), not PTP operator code. Prior successful runs on the same job used payload 4.12.0-0.nightly-2026-04-15-130410; this run used 4.12.0-0.nightly-2026-06-05-032308.

**Affected Images:** 10-8-34-216.sslip.io:5000/openshift/release-images@sha256:9b20418e019c81adf3e553b37405d84a6ee1c62f90902a355195a454df7bbaef (4.12.0-0.nightly-2026-06-05-032308); registry.redhat.io/openshift4/ose-must-gather:latest; ci-telco-runner (ansible/kcli cluster setup). PTP operator images were not exercised — telco5g-ptp-tests step did not execute.

**Is it a flake?** yes — prior 3 builds (2045291541634748416, 2042754953465827328, 2040218314893955072) passed cluster-setup on the same periodic job; only this run failed. Likely bare-metal lab or nightly-payload install instability, not a ptp-operator regression.

**Issue Class:** infra_other

**Related Source Files:**
- https://steps.ci.openshift.org/reference/telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup (cluster setup CI step)
- https://steps.ci.openshift.org/job?org=openshift&repo=release&branch=main&test=e2e-telco5g-ptp&variant=nightly-4.12 (job definition)
- https://prow.ci.openshift.org/view/gs/test-platform-results/logs/periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.12-e2e-telco5g-ptp/2063048605291253760 (failed run)
- https://gcsweb-ci.apps.ci.l2s4.p1.openshiftapps.com/gcs/test-platform-results/logs/periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.12-e2e-telco5g-ptp/2063048605291253760/artifacts/e2e-telco5g-ptp/telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup/build-log.txt (install log)
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go (PTP tests — not reached)

**Suggested Fix:** No ptp-operator code change. Retry the periodic job. If failures recur with nightly-2026-06-05-032308, escalate to telco bare-metal lab admins and openshift/release CI owners to investigate openshift-installer operator bootstrap failure (stuck at 656/831 resources, 78% complete). Compare against last passing payload 4.12.0-0.nightly-2026-04-15-130410.

**Fix Written:** no

**Severity:** MEDIUM

analysis.reason: Test failed: Run multi-stage test e2e-telco5g-ptp - e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests container test
analysis.ai_summary:
**Root Cause:** The telco5g 4.13 CI job runs **main-branch conformance tests** (`TEST_BRANCH=main`) against **release-4.13 component images** (`PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.13`, `USE_UPSTREAM=false`). The four failing tests were added on main in March–April 2026 and require PTP Operator / linuxptp-daemon / cloud-event-proxy behavior that is **not present on release-4.13** (confirmed: commits `0096e6b`, `da824bf`, `6d2f1dd`, `8b697a3` are not ancestors of `release-4.13`).

Two distinct failure mechanisms:

1. **ClockSync “Verify clockClass when locking PTP source” (bc + dualnicbc):** Tests call `EnableGMCapableInPlace()` to patch the PtpConfig CR (`gmCapable 0` → `gmCapable 1`), then poll `/var/run/ptp4l.0.config` for `gmCapable 1` via `WaitForConfigContent()`. On the release-4.13 stack the runtime config never updates within 120s, so the poll times out. Pod logs show clock-class events stuck at value `6` and no config-reload activity during the test window.

2. **Outage recovery “BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage” (bc + dualnicbc):** After `TurnAllPortsDown()`, the test expects the Prometheus clock-class metric to leave Locked (6). With `gmCapable 0` in the BC test config and release-4.13 T-BC clock-class handling, the metric stays at 6. Additionally, `TurnAllPortsDown()`/`TurnAllPortsUp()` did **not** disable NetworkManager (unlike `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty()`, fixed in PR #215), so RHCOS NetworkManager can auto-recover admin-down interfaces and keep the BC locked to its upstream GM.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** The tests that fail on this job were introduced by:
- https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/pull/197 (merged https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/0096e6bacb27afbd208d2101edf41c288c03bcb2) — clockClass locking tests
- https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/pull/185 (merged https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/da824bf082cd75c19fa56a4133902824eb5c2e5e) — BC outage clock-class recovery test

The CI regression is triggered by running these main-only tests on the 4.13 matrix (`TEST_BRANCH=main` + `PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.13` in the build log).

**Affected Images:**
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.13` (built from `release-4.13` in-job)
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.13` (prebuilt release image; not rebuilt when `USE_UPSTREAM=false`)
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.13` (prebuilt release image)

**Is it a flake?** **No.** All four failures are deterministic timeouts on the same assertions. They reproduce because of a version/matrix mismatch and a test helper gap (`TurnAllPortsDown` missing NM handling), not transient infra.

**Issue Class:** **matrix_mismatch** (main-branch tests vs release-4.13 images), with contributing **test_failure** (outage test missing NM disable on bulk port-down).

**Related Source Files:**
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1121 — clockClass locking test (single NIC BC)
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1174 — clockClass locking test (dual NIC BC)
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L2089 — BC outage clock-class recovery test
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go#L596 — `EnableGMCapableInPlace()`
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go#L633 — `WaitForConfigContent()`
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go#L432 — `TurnAllPortsDown()` (missing NM handling)
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go#L1016 — BC config created with `gmCapable 0`
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go#L772 — `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty()` (has NM fix from PR #215)

**Suggested Fix:**
1. **CI (primary):** For `nightly-4.13-e2e-telco5g-ptp`, set `TEST_BRANCH=release-4.13` to match `PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH`, or exclude the new clockClass tests from 4.13 runs until backported.
2. **Tests (in-repo):** Skip T-BC clockClass tests when PTP Operator < 4.16; add `nmcli device set … managed no/yes` to `TurnAllPortsDown()`/`TurnAllPortsUp()` so outage recovery matches the PR #215 behavior.
3. **Longer term:** Backport clockClass test support to release-4.13 only if product owners intend 4.13 to support T-BC clock-class degradation/recovery.

**Fix Written:** **yes** — see `./ci-evidence/fix.patch`
- Adds `IsPTPOperatorVersionAtLeast("4.16")` skips on the three affected test cases
- Adds NetworkManager disable/restore to `TurnAllPortsDown()` / `TurnAllPortsUp()`

**Severity:** **MEDIUM** — affects 4.13 telco5g periodic CI only; core PTP sync tests pass; failures are from running new main-only tests against an older release image set.

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - security.openshift.io
  resourceNames:
  - privileged
  resources:
  - securitycontextconstraints
  verbs:
  - use
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  managedFields:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: privileged-rights
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: builder
  namespace: openshift-ptp
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman
  namespace: openshift-ptp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  serviceAccountName: builder
  containers:
    - name: priv
      image: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4
      command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=PTP_IMAGE
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch OPERATOR_VERSION https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/redhat-cne/cloud-event-proxy.git
          cd cloud-event-proxy
          IMG=${SIDECAR_IMG} make podman-build
          podman push ${SIDECAR_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

      securityContext:
        privileged: true
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push
          name: dockercfg
          readOnly: true
        - name: secret-volume
          mountPath: /root/.docker
  volumes:
    - name: secret-volume
      secret:
        secretName: pull-secret
        items:
        - key: .dockerconfigjson
          path: config.json

    - name: dockercfg
      secret:
        secretName: BUILDER_DOCKERCFG
        defaultMode: 384
'

  name: privileged-rights
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: builder
  namespace: openshift-ptp
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman
  namespace: openshift-ptp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  serviceAccountName: builder
  containers:
    - name: priv
      image: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4
      command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.13
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch release-4.13 https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main bra

analysis.reason: Test failed: Run multi-stage test e2e-telco5g-ptp - e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests container test
analysis.ai_summary:
**Root Cause:** The telco5g-ptp CI job runs **main-branch conformance tests** against **release-4.14** container images (`ptp-operator:4.14`, `linuxptp-daemon:4.14`, `cloud-event-proxy:4.14`) on OCP 4.14.0. Three clock-class tests failed because they assert against the Prometheus metric `openshift_ptp_clock_class`, which on OCP 4.14 does **not** update when ptp4l transitions clock class after upstream sync loss.

CI logs prove ptp4l itself is working: in the locking test, after bringing down slave interface `ens2f3`, **PMC immediately reports clock class 248** while the node-level metric **stays at 6** for the full 5-minute timeout:

```
PMC clock class for /var/run/ptp4l.0.config: 248 (expected 248)
Node-level clock class metric: 6 (expected 248)
```

On OCP 4.14, `openshift_ptp_clock_class` is maintained by **cloud-event-proxy** parsing `CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE` lines from ptp4l logs. When a BC loses sync (with `gmCapable=1`), ptp4l updates its internal parent dataset (visible via PMC) but does not emit the log line cloud-event-proxy needs to refresh the metric. The outage-recovery tests hit the same stale-metric problem; they also use `gmCapable=0` test configs where ptp4l will not degrade clock class on sync loss without an in-place `gmCapable=1` edit.

Additionally, `TurnAllPortsDown` (used by outage tests) did not disable NetworkManager management, unlike `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty` fixed in PR #215 — allowing potential interface auto-recovery during outage simulation.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/210 (commit https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/8b697a3517bc2e24dde547624a96b6d78c4d7a56) — CNF-22878 added node-level `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric verification for OCP < 4.18. That exposed the pre-existing 4.14 cloud-event-proxy limitation where the metric is set once at lock (class 6) but never updated on transition. The outage-recovery test itself was introduced in https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/185.

**Affected Images:**
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.14`
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.14`
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.14`

**Is it a flake?** no — The failure is deterministic. PMC confirms ptp4l transitions correctly; only the Prometheus metric path is broken on 4.14. This is a test-vs-version matrix mismatch, not intermittent infra or timing.

**Issue Class:** matrix_mismatch

**Related Source Files:**
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go#L546-L770 — `VerifyNICClockClass`, PMC helpers, `PortEngine`
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1121-L1168 — clockClass locking test
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L2086-L2170 — outage recovery test
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L3127-L3534 — `checkClockClassState` metric polling
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go#L1016 — BC configs created with `gmCapable 0`
- https://github.com/openshift/cloud-event-proxy/blob/main/plugins/ptp_operator/metrics/ptp4lParse.go#L18-L55 — metric updated only on `CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE` log lines
- https://github.com/openshift/linuxptp-daemon/blob/main/pkg/event/event_tbc.go#L146-L183 — T-BC FSM clock class transitions

**Suggested Fix:** On OCP < 4.18, verify clock class via **PMC** (authoritative ptp4l state) instead of the stale Prometheus metric. For outage-recovery tests, enable `gmCapable=1` in-place (matching real deployments) and disable NetworkManager during `TurnAllPortsDown`/`TurnAllPortsUp` (extending the PR #215 pattern). A product backport to 4.14 cloud-event-proxy to emit metric updates on T-BC state transitions would be a longer-term fix but is out of scope for the test branch.

**Fix Written:** yes — see `./ci-evidence/fix.patch`

Changes made:
1. `VerifyNICClockClass`: PMC-only on OCP < 4.18 (drop stale node-level metric check)
2. `checkClockClassStateReturnBool`: use PMC on OCP < 4.18
3. Outage recovery test: enable `gmCapable=1` before simulating link loss
4. `TurnAllPortsDown`/`TurnAllPortsUp`: disable/re-enable NetworkManager per interface

**Severity:** MEDIUM — PTP sync and clock-class behavior are correct at the ptp4l level; only test assertions against 4.14 metrics are wrong. Does not indicate a production PTP sync failure, but blocks the 4.14 telco5g-ptp CI signal.

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - security.openshift.io
  resourceNames:
  - privileged
  resources:
  - securitycontextconstraints
  verbs:
  - use
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  managedFields:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: privileged-rights
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: builder
  namespace: openshift-ptp
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman
  namespace: openshift-ptp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  serviceAccountName: builder
  containers:
    - name: priv
      image: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4
      command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=PTP_IMAGE
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch OPERATOR_VERSION https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/redhat-cne/cloud-event-proxy.git
          cd cloud-event-proxy
          IMG=${SIDECAR_IMG} make podman-build
          podman push ${SIDECAR_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

      securityContext:
        privileged: true
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push
          name: dockercfg
          readOnly: true
        - name: secret-volume
          mountPath: /root/.docker
  volumes:
    - name: secret-volume
      secret:
        secretName: pull-secret
        items:
        - key: .dockerconfigjson
          path: config.json

    - name: dockercfg
      secret:
        secretName: BUILDER_DOCKERCFG
        defaultMode: 384
'

        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.14
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch release-4.14 https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo
