analysis.ai_summary:
**Root Cause:** Three PTP conformance tests failed in the BC and dualnicbc test modes on OCP 4.15. All failures involve clock-class verification during simulated upstream link loss:

1. **Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on single NIC boundary clock** (bc-serial): PMC correctly reported clockClass 248 after bringing ens2f3 down, but the node-level `openshift_ptp_clock_class` Prometheus metric stayed at 6 for 300s. On OCP < 4.18, cloud-event-proxy exposes only a node-level clock_class metric (no per-config label) that tracks the main clock via `GetMainClockName()`. Per-config freerun transitions are visible via PMC but not reflected in that aggregate metric. PR #210 added node-level metric assertions for OCP < 4.18 single-NIC BC, which is incorrect for freerun (248) checks.

2. **BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage** (bc-serial and dualnicbc-serial): After `TurnAllPortsDown()`, clock class never degraded from Locked (6). NetworkManager on RHCOS auto-recovers admin-down interfaces within ~4–7s, restoring sync before the test could observe degradation. PR #215 fixed this for `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty()` via `nmcli managed=no`, but `TurnAllPortsDown()`/`TurnAllPortsUp()` used by the recovery test were never updated.

**Breaking PR/Commit:**
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/210 (commit https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/8b697a3517bc2e24dde547624a96b6d78c4d7a56) — introduced incorrect node-level metric assertion for freerun on OCP < 4.18
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/185 (commit https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/da824bf082cd75c19fa56a4133902824eb5c2e5e) — added recovery test using `TurnAllPortsDown` without NM guard (incomplete fix in https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/215)

**Affected Images:**
- `openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.15` (operator under test)
- linuxptp-daemon image (`DAEMON_IMAGE` in CI — hosts ptp4l/phc2sys and metrics endpoint)
- cloud-event-proxy sidecar image (`SIDECAR_IMAGE` — publishes `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metrics)
- ptp-operator-conformance-test image (built and run in the telco5g-ptp-tests pod)

**Is it a flake?** no — Failures are deterministic on OCP 4.15: PMC shows correct clock class while node-level metrics do not update for freerun, and NM auto-recovery prevents sustained link outage in tests that lack `nmcli managed=no`.

**Issue Class:** test_regression

**Related Source Files:**
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go#L758-L770 — `VerifyNICClockClass` metrics logic for OCP < 4.18
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go#L432-L468 — `TurnAllPortsDown`/`TurnAllPortsUp` missing NM guard
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1121-L1168 — single-NIC clockClass locking test
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L2086-L2171 — BC clock class recovery test
- https://github.com/openshift/cloud-event-proxy/blob/main/plugins/ptp_operator/metrics/ptp4lParse.go#L32-L54 — node-level metric tracks main clock only

**Suggested Fix:**
1. On OCP < 4.18 single-NIC BC, verify node-level metrics only for locked (6) state; rely on PMC for freerun (248) transitions.
2. Apply `nmcli device set managed no/yes` in `TurnAllPortsDown`/`TurnAllPortsUp` (same pattern as `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty` from PR #215).

**Fix Written:** yes — see `./ci-evidence/fix.patch`

**Severity:** MEDIUM — CI regression on OCP 4.15 telco5g-ptp nightly; operator PTP sync itself works (PMC confirms correct clock class); failures are in test assertions and test harness link-down simulation.

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - security.openshift.io
  resourceNames:
  - privileged
  resources:
  - securitycontextconstraints
  verbs:
  - use
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  managedFields:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: privileged-rights
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: builder
  namespace: openshift-ptp
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman
  namespace: openshift-ptp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  serviceAccountName: builder
  containers:
    - name: priv
      image: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4
      command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=PTP_IMAGE
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch OPERATOR_VERSION https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/redhat-cne/cloud-event-proxy.git
          cd cloud-event-proxy
          IMG=${SIDECAR_IMG} make podman-build
          podman push ${SIDECAR_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

      securityContext:
        privileged: true
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push
          name: dockercfg
          readOnly: true
        - name: secret-volume
          mountPath: /root/.docker
  volumes:
    - name: secret-volume
      secret:
        secretName: pull-secret
        items:
        - key: .dockerconfigjson
          path: config.json

    - name: dockercfg
      secret:
        secretName: BUILDER_DOCKERCFG
        defaultMode: 384
'

          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/redhat-cne/cloud-event-proxy.git
          cd cloud-event-proxy
          IMG=${SIDECAR_IMG} make podman-build
          podman push ${SIDECAR_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

      securityContext:
        privileged: true
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push
          name: dockercfg
          readOnly: true
        - name: secret-volume
          mountPath: /root/.docker
  volumes:
    - name: secret-volume
      secret:
        secretName: pull-secret
        items:
        - key: .dockerconfigjson
          path: config.json

    - name: dockercfg
      secret:
        secretName: BUILDER_DOCKERCFG
        defaultMode: 384'

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - security.openshift.io
  resourceNames:
  - privileged
  resources:
  - securitycontextconstraints
  verbs:
  - use
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  managedFields:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: privileged-rights
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: builder
  namespace: openshift-ptp
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman
  namespace: openshift-ptp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  serviceAccountName: builder
  containers:
    - name: priv
      image: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4
      command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.15
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch release-4.15 https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

analysis.reason: Test failed: Run multi-stage test e2e-telco5g-ptp - e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests container test
analysis.ai_summary:
**Root Cause:** The `periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.16-e2e-telco5g-ptp` job failed because the PTP operator container image was never built or pushed to the cluster registry. During the in-cluster `podman` build step, `make docker-build` on `release-4.16` failed pulling the Dockerfile base image:

```
registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.21-openshift-4.16 → manifest unknown
```

The CI script then incorrectly reported `[INFO] index build succeeded` and continued. `make deploy` created the `ptp-operator` Deployment referencing `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.16`, but that image does not exist. Gather logs confirm the operator pod was stuck in ImagePullBackOff:

```
container "ptp-operator" in pod "ptp-operator-6875dcc7f4-slpfh" is waiting to start: trying and failing to pull image
```

With the operator never running, the `PtpOperatorConfig` reconciler never created the `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet. The test waited the full 400s timeout polling `kubectl rollout status daemonset/linuxptp-daemon -n openshift-ptp`, getting `NotFound` on every iteration, then exited with code 1.

This is **not** a PTP clock-sync or `ptp4l` offset failure — it is a deployment prerequisite failure upstream of all conformance tests.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** No ptp-operator logic regression identified. The `release-4.16` Dockerfile has referenced this builder image for some time:

- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-4.16/Dockerfile#L1

The hermetic-build fix on `release-4.16` (https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/672, commit https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/ff9145755d8931ae60839ab2c42cb2f14ed9bcfb) only changed `GO111MODULE`, not the base image. The likely trigger is **infrastructure**: `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder` tags for older streams (4.16, 4.18) were removed or expired. The same `manifest unknown` error appears on 4.18; 4.17 still has its builder tag and gets past deployment (but fails in functests); 4.19+ switched the telco5g CI build to a public `golang:` base and succeeds.

**Affected Images:**
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.16` — **failed to build/push** (primary)
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.16` — not built in downstream mode (`USE_UPSTREAM=false`)
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.16` — not built in downstream mode
- Operator would have used `quay.io/openshift/origin-ptp:4.16` for the daemon per https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-4.16/config/manager/env.yaml#L16-L21, but the operator never started

**Is it a flake?** **No.** The failure is deterministic: the builder image tag does not exist, the operator image is never pushed, and the DaemonSet is never created. Retrying without fixing the image build will reproduce the same result. Prow history shows the same pattern on 4.16 and 4.18; 4.19/4.21/4.22/4.23 pass.

**Issue Class:** `build_error`

**Related Source Files:**
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-4.16/Dockerfile#L1 — references unavailable builder image
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-4.16/config/manager/env.yaml#L16-L21 — daemon/sidecar image env vars set at deploy
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/controllers/ptpoperatorconfig_controller.go#L220-L301 — `syncLinuxptpDaemon()` creates the DaemonSet (never reached)
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/bindata/manifests/linuxptp/ptp-daemon.yaml — DaemonSet manifest template
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go#L477-L489 — in-repo test helper that waits for DaemonSet existence (telco5g CI uses its own `retry_with_timeout` shell wrapper instead)
- Telco5g CI image-build script (in `openshift/release`, not this repo) — masks build failure and proceeds to deploy

**Suggested Fix:**
1. **Backport to `release-4.16`:** Update `Dockerfile` to use a publicly available builder base (as `release-4.19` does with `golang:1.22.4`) instead of `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.21-openshift-4.16`. Reference: https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-4.19/Dockerfile#L1
2. **Fix telco5g CI script** (in `openshift/release`): Fail the job when `make docker-build` or `podman push` fails; do not print "index build succeeded" and continue. Add a `kubectl rollout status deployment/ptp-operator` gate before waiting on the DaemonSet.
3. **Alternative:** For EOL streams, skip in-cluster image builds and use pre-built ART images from `quay.io/openshift/origin-ptp-operator:4.16`.

**Fix Written:** **no** — the fix must land on `release-4.16` and/or the `openshift/release` CI config; this workspace tracks `main`, which already uses a different Dockerfile (`ubi9/go-toolset:1.25`) and is not what the 4.16 nightly job builds.

**Severity:** **HIGH** — the entire `e2e-telco5g-ptp` suite for OCP 4.16 is blocked at operator deployment; no PTP functionality is exercised. Lower than CRITICAL because 4.16 is a maintenance-stream nightly and newer versions (4.19+) are passing.

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - security.openshift.io
  resourceNames:
  - privileged
  resources:
  - securitycontextconstraints
  verbs:
  - use
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  managedFields:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: privileged-rights
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: builder
  namespace: openshift-ptp
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman
  namespace: openshift-ptp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  serviceAccountName: builder
  containers:
    - name: priv
      image: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4
      command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=PTP_IMAGE
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch OPERATOR_VERSION https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/redhat-cne/cloud-event-proxy.git
          cd cloud-event-proxy
          IMG=${SIDECAR_IMG} make podman-build
          podman push ${SIDECAR_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

      securityContext:
        privileged: true
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push
          name: dockercfg
          readOnly: true
        - name: secret-volume
          mountPath: /root/.docker
  volumes:
    - name: secret-volume
      secret:
        secretName: pull-secret
        items:
        - key: .dockerconfigjson
          path: config.json

    - name: dockercfg
      secret:
        secretName: BUILDER_DOCKERCFG
        defaultMode: 384
'
++ sed s#OPERATOR_VERSION#release-4.16#

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1

  namespace: openshift-ptp
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman
  namespace: openshift-ptp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  serviceAccountName: builder
  containers:
    - name: priv
      image: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4
      command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.16
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch release-4.16 https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
